fix(loop): persist commit_nudged across TUI run() invocations

The TUI calls loop::run() once per user turn, so a local
`commit_nudged` variable reset to false every message — causing
the "you made file changes" nudge to fire on every turn even
after a successful commit.

Move commit_nudged onto IntentDocument (which persists for the
entire session lifetime). Also reset it when the commit tool
clears files_modified, so a second commit after further changes
still gets a nudge.

Fixes: commit nudge regression introduced in rc.7"

feat(docs): Write user-facing documentation about Anthropic subscription ToS compliance. Two deliverables: (1) A new markdown doc at docs/anthropic-subscription-tos.md covering: what the restriction is (exact ToS quote), which Omegon entry points are allowed vs blocked, the interactive-vs-automated distinction, what to do if you need automation (get an API key), and how this compares to other providers (GitHub Copilot has no such restriction). Include a clear table: TUI mode=allowed, --initial-prompt=allowed, --prompt/--prompt-file=blocked, --smoke=blocked, /cleave=blocked. Include the exact Anthropic ToS URL. Make it factual and non-alarmist — this is a clear boundary that Omegon enforces for the operator's protection. (2) A new Astro page at site/src/pages/docs/providers.astro covering all provider auth modes: Anthropic API key (unrestricted), Anthropic OAuth/subscription (interactive TUI only), OpenAI API key, Codex OAuth, Ollama (local, unrestricted), GitHub Models (official PAT, coming soon), Copilot seat (coming soon). For each: how to configure, what's allowed, any restrictions. Follow the structure and style of existing pages like site/src/pages/docs/quickstart.astro or site/src/pages/docs/security.astro. Files: docs/anthropic-subscription-tos.md, site/src/pages/docs/providers.astro
